replies.author: @deannaprince7954
replies.text:
​@gatordonttakenoshiat Charisma on Command did an awesome video long ago, that has the opposite perspective.  He pointed out that Ferguson would test waters, then pull back or continue, based on the woman's reactions.  That he'd let her comfort level determine his approach.  And that he would always keep it self-deprecating.

I LOVED Ferguson's show, & his delivery & timing, & especially his rapport with guests.  Everything has been downhill since him.  Is he skeevy?  Personally, I could go either way.

I can easily & comfortably defend his flirting as playing a part, playing up the raunchy side of his humor.  But if he's a skeevy guy IRL, I can just as easily condemn it as inappropriate.

Really, to me, a lot of Ferguson's humor is relational/transactional, & thus depends on whether he himself is a man of good character.  He unabashedly loves America, & that's been good enough for me.  I could be wrong, tho.

author: @EthalaRide
text:
Also, something that kind of got overlooked was James basically copped Carpool Kareoke from *Flula Borg,* (real name Kristof Robinson), a German comedian, actor, musician, and YouTuber who you've probably seen in pretty popular movies and TV like The Suicide Squad, Pitch Perfect 2, Boss Baby, The Rookie, and Conan would interview him. Flula used to interview people, actors, celebrities, internet personalities, and his very off-beat Euro-DJ bold 'simpleton' personality gave his interviews a between-the-ferns hilarious awkwardness.
The dude is a sleeper talent, not gonna lie, and he always maintains his 'German exchange student' clown persona, but is clearly incredibly talented and well-liked by the people he meets.

Flula had a segment on his YouTube Channel called _"Auto Tunes."_ He would do everything in one take, live audio, real driving, and would incorporate different people he'd interviewed. He'd use his DJ and improv skills to record and mix cover songs live *in his beater car,* while either alone and parked, or with a friend ACTUALLY DRIVING (AUTO tunes, like Automobile, because he's German). 


I'm absolutely sure that James either ripped off the concept of Carpool Karaoke from Flula or perhaps the concept was bought, paid for, and brought to James by the executives to try out. So even THAT might not be original.

replies.author: @yealandred
replies.text: I agree Flula is great (and better) but Carpool Karaoke started as an older idea that he did on British TV before taking it to the US so it's probably coincidence. author: @paulnash9851
text:
With regards Sir Patrick Stewart.
I did volunteer work at a small arts centre/theatre in a rural area.
Sir Patrick was there as the lead in a production.
It was a big deal both financially + publicity wise for us a small place.
The man was an absolute class act.
Had time for everybody, no matter who.
Was brilliantly funny, witty + polite while always taking an interest in the other person.
He knew every single person there by first name after the first day + always cheerily greeted + thanked each one.
He went out of his way to do extra + anything that could benefit the centre.
He was the same to everyone at the last night/wrap party.
An absolute gentleman, massive talent + just a great fucking guy.
He earned the right to obliterate Corden like that, because he absolutely isn’t like that.
Top man that Sir Patrick, top man... 👍👌

A good friend wife's grandfather used to (may still actually, I haven't asked since and this was about a decade ago) play bridge with his brother and she accidentally met him as a consequence a few times and she only echoed what you had to say about him here. 

Evidently he was a stand out fella, not only taking a genuine interest in her personal life (and remembered the details when they next met, she was engaged to my friend the first time and he asked her how the wedding went, even asking if she had any photos) but was a full patron of the bridge club despite not really being a member and only visiting the place a couple of times a year at most. He also gave to them quite generously whenever they had a whip round for repairs and restoration of the building (it originally being a 17th century coaching inn and as a result a listed building meant it was not only repair prone but the work had to be done to set rules and standards). 

I only regret that I never asked her to put the word forward that whenever it was convenient I would quite like to have his autograph, being a considerable fan of Star Trek and TNG especially.

author: @lunaderacine
text:
I saw James at his show as an audience member once a long time ago, not that I wanted to as I really didn't like him even then (went there to see Dave Grohl) I saw for myself how shitty he is with his audience. Completely ignored people who were screaming for him during breaks, instead he'd walk to his desk and browse on his phone looking so grumpy and bored like he'd rather be anywhere else. I found it disgusting that he wouldn't even bat an eye to his audience who obviously want acknowledgement, but no, absolutely nothing. He looked annoyed the whole time he was off camera. Ohh but when the camera is back on THEN he goes back to his smiley TV persona and only THEN he acknowledges his audience, like a massive hypocrite douchebag. Glad his nastiness is more well known now!

I had gone to see Craig Ferguson while he was there as host of the Late Late Show a few years prior to that (Larry King was the guest) and it was such a more positive experience. He was himself all throughout, during breaks he would chat and joke with his audience, shook hands with as many people as he could, and signed autographs. He was such an awesome and funny guy, it's a real shame he left the show only for someone like James to fill his seat. 🙄
